page: Microsoft Azure E20ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the EPSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for attaching external block storage.

Benchmark results place the E20ps_v5 in the average performance tier for most multi-core and memory-bound operations. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 36041.2 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10292.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1406.22 GB/sec and an uncached large block read bandwidth of 131.23 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core (1100.0) and single-threaded bzip3 decompression (8.31 GB/sec) scoring in the bottom 25% tier.

Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated ARM cores, the E20ps_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX. It is well-suited for database operations, as demonstrated by its Passmark database score of 13196.44 and Redis SET performance of 27.33 million operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded text and image processing, and lightweight LLM inference using smaller models.
description: Microsoft Azure E20ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the EPSv5 family, configured with 20 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 160.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 36041.2 and a Redis SET throughput of 27.33 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is a tradeoff, with its Geekbench single-core score falling into the bottom 25% tier. This server type is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads such as database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing that benefit from dedicated ARM cores without requiring x86 instruction sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores and 160 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 20 dedicated cores and 160 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated physical cores for memory-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 20 dedicated Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, offering 8.0 GB of memory per core
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or integrated GPUs
Delivers 27.33 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for database hosting, static web serving, and memory-intensive ARM workloads
